solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1571E - Fix the String
Firstly, let's notice that there are only two correct bracket sequences of length $$$4$$$:
(())
and
()()
.
Let's look at the neighboring ones (i.e., there are no other ones between them) in the string $$$a$$$. Let their positions be $$$i$$$ and $$$j$$$ ($$$j  \lt  i$$$). Let's consider what restrictions they put on each other:
if $$$i - j = 1$$$, then there is no answer, because it is impossible to choose a pair of correct bracket sequences that the last $$$3$$$ characters of one coincide with the first $$$3$$$ characters of the other;
if $$$i - j = 2$$$, then the only option when the last $$$2$$$ characters of one coincide with the $$$2$$$ characters of the other is two sequences of the form
()()
.
if $$$i - j = 3$$$, then there is no answer, because it is impossible to choose a pair of correct bracket sequences that the last character of one coincides with the first character of the other;
otherwise, they do not put any restrictions on each other.
Thus, for all $$$i$$$ that $$$a_i = 1$$$, we know what the correct bracket sequence should be the substring $$$s[i;i+3]$$$. If both sequences are suitable, then you have to choose the one that changes the minimum number of positions.
